title: Group Travel Near Hatsudai: A Whole House Between Shinjuku and Shibuya
excerpt: Why an entire private house at Plat House Hatsudai beats splitting up across hotel rooms for groups of up to 4.
contentHtml:
Sandwiched between the culture of Hatsudai and the energy of Shinjuku, this pocket of the city is quiet enough to sleep well and connected enough to reach almost anywhere in Tokyo without much effort. For groups traveling together, that combination is hard to beat.

An Entire House, Not Just a Room

Plat House Hatsudai is a full 30㎡ house across two floors, fitted with two double mattresses and comfortably sleeping up to 4 guests. Instead of booking multiple hotel rooms and splitting up for the night, everyone shares one space — with a living area, kitchen, and bathroom that belong entirely to your group. It's a 7-minute walk from Hatsudai Station and 10 minutes from Nishi-Shinjuku-Gochome Station, putting the whole Shinjuku area within easy reach.

Set Up for a Real Stay, Not Just a Night

The kitchen is fully equipped for actual cooking, and a drum washing machine and dryer mean laundry doesn't pile up over a longer trip. Netflix, a Nintendo Switch, and even a PlayStation are on hand for downtime, while a bathtub and washlet-equipped bathroom add comfort that budget accommodations often skip. Air purifier, humidifier, and circulator round out a surprisingly complete list of amenities for a private rental this size.

Close to Both Culture and Nightlife

Tokyo Opera City and the New National Theatre are practically next door, making this a natural base for an evening at the theater. Shibuya's scramble crossing and Meiji Shrine are both a short ride away, so you can spend the day in Shibuya and still be back in a quiet, residential pocket of the city by night.

Book Direct and Save

Booking directly with Plat House Hatsudai gets you a better rate than OTA listings, with free cancellation up to 7 days before check-in.

Check Availability & Book Now
